articles.title: New Chrome Zero-Day (CVE-2026-2441) Under Active Attack — Patch Released
articles.summary: Google on Friday released security updates for its Chrome browser to address a security flaw that it said has been exploited in the wild.  The high-severity vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-2441  (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as a use-after-free bug in CSS. Security researcher Shaheen Fazim has been credited with discovering and reporting the shortcoming on February 11, 2026.  "Use after free in CSS in Google Chrome prior to 145.0.7632.75 allowed a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code inside a sandbox via a crafted HTML page," according to a description of the flaw in the NIST's National Vulnerability Database (NVD).  Google did not disclose any details about how the vulnerability is being exploited in the wild, by whom, or who may have been targeted, but it acknowledged  that "an exploit for CVE-2026-2441 exists in the wild."   While Google Chrome is no stranger to actively exploited vulnerabilities, the development once again highlights how bro...
articles.title_fr: Nouvelle faille zero-day Chrome (CVE-2026-2441) sous attaque active — Correctif publié
articles.summary_fr: Google a publié des mises à jour de sécurité pour son navigateur Chrome afin de corriger une faille zero-day de haute gravité (CVE-2026-2441), déjà exploitée dans la nature. Décrite comme une vulnérabilité de type "use-after-free" dans CSS, elle permet à un attaquant d'exécuter du code arbitraire via une page HTML malveillante. Bien que Google n'ait pas divulgué les détails de l'exploitation, la société a confirmé l'existence d'un exploit actif.

articles.title: Microsoft Discloses DNS-Based ClickFix Attack Using Nslookup for Malware Staging
articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed  details of a new version of the ClickFix social engineering tactic in which the attackers trick unsuspecting users into running commands that carry out a Domain Name System (DNS) lookup to retrieve the next-stage payload.  Specifically, the attack relies on using the " nslookup " (short for nameserver lookup ) command to execute a custom DNS lookup triggered via the Windows Run dialog.  ClickFix is an increasingly popular technique  that's traditionally delivered via phishing, malvertising, or drive-by download schemes, often redirecting targets to bogus landing pages that host fake CAPTCHA verification or instructions to address a non-existent problem on their computers by running a command either through the Windows Run dialog or the macOS Terminal app.  The attack method has become widespread over the past two years since it hinges on the victims infecting their own machines with malware, thereby allowing the threat actors to bypass security c...
articles.title_fr: Microsoft révèle une attaque ClickFix basée sur DNS utilisant Nslookup pour le déploiement de malwares
articles.summary_fr: Microsoft a détaillé une nouvelle variante de l'attaque d'ingénierie sociale ClickFix, où les utilisateurs sont incités à exécuter des commandes DNS pour récupérer des charges utiles de malwares. Cette méthode exploite spécifiquement la commande "nslookup" via la boîte de dialogue Exécuter de Windows pour effectuer une recherche DNS personnalisée. Devenue courante, cette technique permet aux victimes d'infecter elles-mêmes leurs machines, contournant ainsi les contrôles de sécurité traditionnels.

articles.title: Google Ties Suspected Russian Actor to CANFAIL Malware Attacks on Ukrainian Orgs
articles.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to attacks targeting Ukrainian organizations with malware known as CANFAIL .  Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) described the hacking group as possibly affiliated with Russian intelligence services. The threat actor is assessed to have targeted defense, military, government, and energy organizations within the Ukrainian regional and national governments.  However, the group has also exhibited growing interest in aerospace organizations, manufacturing companies with military and drone ties, nuclear and chemical research organizations, and international organizations involved in conflict monitoring and humanitarian aid in Ukraine, GTIG added.   "Despite being less sophisticated and resourced than other Russian threat groups, this actor recently began to overcome some technical limitations using LLMs [large language models]," GTIG said .   "Through prompting, they conduct reconnaissance, create lures for soci...
articles.title_fr: Google relie un acteur russe présumé aux attaques de malware CANFAIL contre des organisations ukrainiennes
articles.summary_fr: Google attribue à un acteur de menace non documenté, potentiellement lié aux services de renseignement russes, des attaques contre des organisations ukrainiennes utilisant le malware CANFAIL. Ce groupe cible principalement les secteurs de la défense, militaire, gouvernemental et énergétique, mais montre également un intérêt croissant pour l'aérospatiale et la recherche. Malgré des ressources limitées, cet acteur a commencé à utiliser des modèles de langage (LLM) pour la reconnaissance et la création de leurres, améliorant ainsi ses capacités.
